title: alchemilla alpina subsp. glomerata
intro: Alchemilla alpina subsp. glomerata, also known as the Alpine Lady's Mantle, is a plant species in the Rosaceae family, characterized by its distinctive umbrella-like inflorescences. The medicinal properties of Alchemilla alpina subsp. glomerata lie in its high content of flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, as well as its potential to help manage conditions such as eczema, acne, and gout. Alchemilla alpina subsp. glomerata can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine to support skin health and reduce inflammation.
